name: Rice
crop_cycle_note: Annual crop; duration varies 100-150 days depending on variety (short/medium/long duration)

growth_stages.name: Nursery Stage
characteristics: Grown in dense nursery bed before transplanting
care_focus: Maintain thin water film in nursery bed
Watch for seedling blight in overcrowded beds

growth_stages.name: Vegetative / Tillering
characteristics: Active tiller production, plant bushy in appearance
Leaf color deep green if nitrogen adequate
care_focus: Maintain 2-5 cm standing water for tillering
First and second nitrogen top-dressing during this window

growth_stages.name: Reproductive / Panicle Initiation
characteristics: Stem elongation begins, young panicle visible when stem dissected
Tiller number stabilizes, unproductive tillers may die back
care_focus: Critical stage for nitrogen top-dressing (panicle initiation dose)
Maintain consistent water level, avoid drought stress now

growth_stages.name: Flowering / Heading
characteristics: Panicle emerges fully from the leaf sheath
Flowering (anthesis) occurs, most sensitive stage to stress
care_focus: Avoid water stress and pesticide spraying during flower opening hours
Monitor closely for gundhi bug (rice bug) and neck blast

growth_stages.name: Grain Filling (Milk / Dough Stage)
characteristics: Grains filling with milky then doughy content
Panicles bending downward as grain weight increases
care_focus: Maintain shallow water, then begin gradual drainage as grains harden
Protect from birds and rodent damage

growth_stages.name: Maturity / Harvest
characteristics: Grains hard, straw turns golden yellow
80-85% of grains in the panicle should be straw-colored for harvest
care_focus: Drain field fully 7-10 days before harvest
Timely harvest to avoid grain shattering and lodging losses

operations: Main sowing/transplanting season with monsoon onset
Maintain field bunds to retain water during rains
Apply basal fertilizer dose at transplanting
precautions: High risk for blast and bacterial leaf blight in continuous wet weather
Ensure drainage outlets to avoid complete submergence during heavy rain

operations: Second crop cycle in irrigated areas; sowing/transplanting in Nov-Dec
Careful water scheduling since rainfall is minimal
precautions: Cold spells during flowering can cause spikelet sterility
Watch for brown planthopper buildup in dense, over-irrigated fields

operations: Summer/zaid crop only in assured irrigation areas
Frequent irrigation needed due to high evapotranspiration
precautions: Heat stress during flowering can reduce grain set
Stem borer incidence often higher in summer crop

diseases.name: Rice Blast
symptoms: Spindle/diamond-shaped lesions with grey center and brown margin on leaves
Node blast causes blackening and breaking at the node
Neck blast causes the panicle neck to rot, resulting in empty grains
treatment: Spray tricyclazole or a recommended systemic fungicide at first appearance
Avoid excess nitrogen application which increases susceptibility
Drain excess standing water temporarily to reduce humidity around the crop
prevention: Balanced fertilization, avoid excess nitrogen doses
Seed treatment with fungicide before sowing

diseases.name: Bacterial Leaf Blight (BLB)
symptoms: Water-soaked lesions starting from leaf tip/margin turning yellow to white/grey
Wavy margin of lesion distinguishes it from blast
Whole leaf can dry up in severe infection (kresek phase in seedlings)
treatment: Spray a copper-based bactericide or streptocycline-copper mixture
Drain field to reduce humidity, avoid excess nitrogen
Remove and destroy severely infected plants in seedling stage
prevention: Avoid clipping seedling tops during transplanting in endemic areas

diseases.name: Sheath Blight
symptoms: Oval, greenish-grey lesions on leaf sheath near the water line
Lesions enlarge with irregular brown margins, coalescing upward
Lodging risk in severe, dense crop stands
treatment: Spray validamycin or a recommended fungicide targeting Rhizoctonia
Reduce planting density in future sowing to improve air flow
Avoid excess nitrogen which promotes dense canopy microclimate
prevention: Clean field bunds of weed hosts

diseases.name: Brown Spot
symptoms: Small oval brown spots with grey center scattered across the leaf
Common in nutrient-deficient soils, especially low potassium/silicon
Can also infect grains causing discoloration
treatment: Spray mancozeb or a recommended fungicide
Apply potash fertilizer to correct deficiency

diseases.name: False Smut
symptoms: Individual grains in the panicle transformed into greenish-yellow velvety spore balls
More common in high nitrogen, dense canopy fields during humid weather
treatment: Spray a recommended fungicide (e.g. propiconazole) at panicle initiation to booting stage as preventive measure
Hand-remove and destroy visible smut balls to reduce spread to storage

pests.name: Yellow Stem Borer
symptoms: Dead heart (central shoot dries up) in vegetative stage
White ear/whitehead (empty panicle) at reproductive stage
management: Install pheromone traps to monitor and mass-trap adult moths
Apply recommended granular insecticide at early tillering if infestation crosses threshold
Clip and destroy egg masses on leaf tips when seen

pests.name: Brown Planthopper (BPH)
symptoms: Circular patches of yellowing and drying plants (hopper burn)
Insects visible at the base of the plant near the water line
management: Drain field intermittently to disrupt breeding conditions
Spray a recommended insecticide targeted at the base of the plant if hopper burn patches appear

pests.name: Rice Leaf Folder
symptoms: Leaves folded longitudinally with larvae feeding inside, leaving white streaks
Scraped, transparent patches visible when leaf unfolded
management: Field sanitation - remove grassy weeds that host the pest
Spray a recommended insecticide if folding crosses 10-15% of leaves

pests.name: Rice Gall Midge
symptoms: Central shoot transforms into a hollow, onion-like 'silver shoot' gall
Affected tillers do not produce panicles
management: Use resistant varieties in endemic tracts
Remove and destroy silver shoots when first noticed
Avoid staggered planting dates across a region which sustains midge populations

pests.name: Gundhi Bug (Rice Bug)
symptoms: Chaffy, empty, or partially filled grains from sap sucking during milk stage
Characteristic foul odor from disturbed bugs in the field
management: Field and bund sanitation to remove grassy weed hosts
Spray a recommended insecticide during early morning or evening when bugs are most active

risk_conditions.impact: Elevated blast and bacterial leaf blight risk
Spikelet sterility, reduced tillering, stress-related yield loss
Poor grain set if occurring during flowering stage
Spikelet sterility and delayed maturity in winter (rabi) crop
Crop lodging near maturity causing harvest losses
